symptoms.symptom_name: Inflammatory back pain (prolonged morning stiffness, better with movement, bilateral SI)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged morning stiffness with pain better on movement and bilateral SI involvement is inflammatory-pattern axial disease.
Mechanical pain is usually worse with movement and lacks prolonged morning stiffness.

symptoms.symptom_name: No saddle numbness or bladder/bowel dysfunction (cauda equina screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of saddle anaesthesia and sphincter disturbance makes cauda equina very unlikely, but it must be explicitly screened and safety-netted.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new leg weakness or below-knee sciatica

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No unilateral below-knee sciatica argues against a compressive disc radiculopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Maxed OTC analgesia with no effect

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Poor response to standard analgesia often needs a prescription NSAID; also flags NSAID overuse risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: High fall risk from axial rigidity, isolated home with hazards

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe rigidity with pain and floor hazards while alone creates a fall risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe paraspinal spasm, restricted Schober, bilateral SI tenderness, intact neuro/perianal sensation (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Restricted lumbar flexion with SI tenderness and intact neurology fits axial inflammatory disease.
Intact perianal sensation/neurology argues against cauda equina.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Inflammatory-pattern back pain with bilateral SI involvement, a negative cauda-equina screen and no radiculopathy, on known axial PsA, is an inflammatory axial flare — but cauda equina can't be examined remotely, so urgent in-person review (± MRI) with cauda-equina safety-netting, NSAID optimisation and fall-safety is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude cauda equina/cord compression; cannot be done via chat.
To assess active sacroiliitis and exclude structural compression if concern arises.
To assess inflammation and progress DMARD/biologic planning.
